_meta.purpose: Staining / preparation methods per laboratory discipline -> principle, use, key result, interpretation. Feeds the AI interpretation + RAG so the model knows the correct stain for each module (fungi vs parasitology vs histology vs cytology differ). Decision support; the SOP + a qualified scientist govern practice.
_meta.source: Standard laboratory methods (Bailey & Scott, Dacie & Lewis, Bancroft histotechnology, CDC DPDx, WHO).

wright_giemsa.name: Wright–Giemsa (Romanowsky)
wright_giemsa.principle: Polychrome eosin–methylene blue; acidic vs basic cell components stain differently
wright_giemsa.use: Peripheral blood + bone marrow smears — cell morphology, differential, parasites
wright_giemsa.interpretation: Nucleus purple, cytoplasm pink/blue; basis of the manual differential and PBS review
wright_giemsa.note: pH 6.8 buffer critical; too-blue = alkaline, too-pink = acidic.

perls_prussian_blue.name: Perls' Prussian blue (iron)
perls_prussian_blue.principle: Ferric iron + potassium ferrocyanide -> blue ferric ferrocyanide
perls_prussian_blue.use: Marrow iron stores, ring sideroblasts, siderocytes
perls_prussian_blue.interpretation: Absent stainable iron -> iron-deficiency; ring sideroblasts (>=15%) -> sideroblastic anaemia / MDS-RS

reticulocyte_nmb.name: Reticulocyte (new methylene blue / brilliant cresyl blue)
reticulocyte_nmb.principle: Supravital precipitation of residual ribosomal RNA
reticulocyte_nmb.interpretation: High retic -> haemolysis/blood loss response; low -> hypoproliferative (e.g. aplasia, deficiency)

mpo.name: Myeloperoxidase (MPO)
mpo.principle: Peroxidase in primary granules oxidises substrate
mpo.interpretation: MPO+ -> myeloid (AML); MPO-negative blasts -> lymphoid (ALL) — correlate with flow

pas_heme.name: PAS (haematology)
pas_heme.principle: Periodic acid oxidises glycogen -> Schiff magenta
pas_heme.interpretation: Block/coarse PAS positivity -> lymphoblasts (ALL) or erythroid precursors

gram.name: Gram stain
gram.principle: Crystal violet–iodine retained by thick peptidoglycan after decolourisation
gram.use: Bacteria + yeast on smears (pus, CSF, sputum...)
gram.interpretation: Gram-positive purple / Gram-negative pink; morphology + arrangement guide empiric therapy
gram.note: Over-decolourisation makes GPos read as GNeg.

ziehl_neelsen.name: Ziehl–Neelsen (AFB, hot)
ziehl_neelsen.principle: Carbol fuchsin retained by mycolic-acid wall despite acid-alcohol
ziehl_neelsen.interpretation: Acid-fast bacilli red on blue background; grade Neg/Scanty/1+/2+/3+

auramine_rhodamine.name: Auramine–rhodamine (fluorochrome)
auramine_rhodamine.interpretation: AFB fluoresce yellow-green; confirm positives by ZN

albert.name: Albert's stain
albert.interpretation: Green bacilli with bluish-black metachromatic (Babes–Ernst) granules

india_ink.name: India ink (negative)
india_ink.interpretation: Clear halo (capsule) around budding yeast -> cryptococcal meningitis (confirm CrAg)

spore_stain.name: Spore stain (Schaeffer–Fulton)
spore_stain.principle: Malachite green driven into spores with heat

koh.name: KOH wet mount (+/- DMSO)
koh.principle: KOH digests keratin/host cells, leaving fungal elements
koh.use: Skin/nail/hair, sputum, any specimen for hyphae/yeast
koh.interpretation: Septate/aseptate hyphae, budding yeast, pseudohyphae, spherules

lactophenol_cotton_blue.name: Lactophenol cotton blue (LPCB)
lactophenol_cotton_blue.principle: Cotton blue stains chitin; lactophenol preserves + clears
lactophenol_cotton_blue.interpretation: Blue hyphae + conidia/spore morphology -> genus (Aspergillus, Penicillium...)

calcofluor_white.name: Calcofluor white
calcofluor_white.interpretation: Fungal walls fluoresce bright blue-white under UV

gms.name: Gomori methenamine silver (GMS)
gms.principle: Silver impregnation of fungal cell-wall polysaccharides
gms.interpretation: Black fungal elements on green background

pas_fungi.name: PAS (fungi)
pas_fungi.interpretation: Magenta hyphae/yeast; good for dimorphic fungi

saline_wet_mount.name: Saline wet mount
saline_wet_mount.principle: Motility + refractile morphology in isotonic saline
saline_wet_mount.use: Stool O&P — trophozoite motility, larvae, ova

iodine_wet_mount.name: Lugol's iodine wet mount
iodine_wet_mount.interpretation: Highlights nuclei/karyosome + glycogen of protozoan cysts (kills motility)

trichrome.name: Trichrome (Wheatley/Gomori)
trichrome.interpretation: Cytoplasm blue-green, nuclei/inclusions red — Entamoeba, Giardia detail

modified_ziehl_neelsen.name: Modified Ziehl–Neelsen (cold/Kinyoun)
modified_ziehl_neelsen.interpretation: Red acid-fast oocysts on blue; size distinguishes species

giemsa_thick_thin.name: Giemsa (thick + thin films)
giemsa_thick_thin.interpretation: Thick = detection + parasitaemia; thin = species + stage; pH 7.2 buffer for stippling

kato_katz.name: Kato–Katz (prep, not a stain)
kato_katz.interpretation: Eggs per gram -> WHO intensity category

h_and_e.name: Haematoxylin & Eosin (H&E)
h_and_e.principle: Haematoxylin (basic) -> nuclei blue; eosin (acidic) -> cytoplasm pink
h_and_e.interpretation: Baseline for all histopathology; malignancy features assessed on H&E first

pas_histo.name: PAS (+/- diastase)
pas_histo.interpretation: Diastase removes glycogen -> distinguishes glycogen from other PAS+ material

congo_red.name: Congo red
congo_red.interpretation: Apple-green birefringence under polarised light = amyloid

masson_trichrome.name: Masson trichrome
masson_trichrome.interpretation: Collagen blue/green, muscle/cytoplasm red -> quantify fibrosis

papanicolaou.name: Papanicolaou (Pap)
papanicolaou.principle: Polychrome; nuclear haematoxylin + OG-6/EA cytoplasmic counterstains
papanicolaou.interpretation: Nuclear detail + keratinisation for Bethesda reporting (NILM/ASC-US/LSIL/HSIL/SCC)
papanicolaou.note: Requires WET fixation (95% ethanol) immediately — air-drying ruins nuclear detail.

mgg.name: May–Grünwald–Giemsa (MGG)
mgg.interpretation: Complements Pap; better for cytoplasm, background, organisms

diff_quik.name: Diff-Quik
diff_quik.interpretation: Immediate adequacy + preliminary read at the bedside

h_and_e_cyto.name: H&E (cell block)
h_and_e_cyto.principle: As histology on the cell block
h_and_e_cyto.interpretation: Architecture + enables IHC panels on cytology material
